segments.text: Let's start with the market validation update. We ran the surveys last week across three segments.
The enterprise segment showed strong interest, but I'm not sure about the SMB numbers. They looked inflated to me.
Fair point. The SMB sample size was only forty-two respondents. We need at least a hundred for statistical significance.
I ran the unit economics on the enterprise tier and the margins are incredible. Seventy-eight percent gross margin after infrastructure costs.
That assumes we hit the projected customer acquisition cost. If CAC doubles, which it easily could in this market, those margins evaporate.
Good challenge. Let's model the sensitivity analysis. What's our break-even CAC?
Break-even is around twelve hundred per customer. Current projection is eight-fifty, so we have a forty percent buffer.
That buffer assumes the current conversion rates hold. We've only been running paid acquisition for two weeks. I'd wait for sixty-day data before committing to those projections.
Agreed. Let's flag the CAC assumption as unvalidated and revisit in the next board update. Moving on to the product roadmap.
One more thing on unit economics -- the Tier 2 pricing experiment exceeded expectations. We should double down on that before the enterprise push.
